title: Neomammillaria canelensis
intro: Neomammillaria canelensis, also known as the Cane Cholla, is a type of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria canelensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as isopimarane, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria canelensis is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. The genus Neomammillaria is now considered a synonym of Mammillaria, so N. canelensis is often referred to as Mammillaria canelensis. It is a type of mammillated cactus, characterized by its small, globular stems and short, fine spines. The classification of N. canelensis is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.
